summary: Closed, unmerged PR that attempted to make `src/sysdolphin/baselib/spline.c` matching. The diff changed linkage/inline qualifiers, adjusted local variable structure in `splArcLengthGetParameter`, introduced a tiny helper for arc-length dx calculation, updated `.sdata2` symbol names, removed an exported prototype for now-static `splGetBSplinePoint`, and flipped `spline.c` from `NonMatching` to `Matching` in `configure.py`. The decomp.dev bot reported 2 new GALE01 matches: `splArcLengthGetParameter` reached 100.00% and spline `.sdata2` reached 100.00%, with +912 matched code bytes and +56 matched data bytes.
what_changed: `splGetCardinalPoint` and `splGetBezierPoint` were changed from plain `void` definitions to `inline void` definitions.
`splGetBSplinePoint` was changed to `static void`, and its public declaration was removed from `src/sysdolphin/baselib/spline.h`.
`splArcLengthPolynomial` was changed from `inline f32` to `static f32`.
A new helper `static f32 spl_GetArcLengthDx(f32 start, f32 midpoint)` was added to return `(midpoint - start) / 8.0F`.

`splArcLengthGetParameter` was reshaped by moving the `result` declaration, replacing a `const f32 midpoint` temporary with assignment to `result`, computing `dx` through `spl_GetArcLengthDx`, and using `result` in the Simpson integration and bisection updates.

`config/GALE01/symbols.txt` replaced named float/double literals like `HSD_Spline_804DE620` through `HSD_Spline_804DE654` with compiler-style `@13`, `@14`, `@15`, `@17`, `@18`, `@19`, `@80`, `@82`, `@91`, `@101`, `@178`, and `@179` labels.
smart_moves: Used linkage and inlining qualifiers as matching levers instead of changing spline math behavior.
Made `splGetBSplinePoint` internal-only in both the C file and header, keeping declarations consistent with the new `static` definition.
Extracted the `(midpoint - start) / 8.0F` expression into `spl_GetArcLengthDx`, likely to steer codegen/register scheduling in the sensitive `splArcLengthGetParameter` loop.
Replaced the separate `midpoint` const with the already-declared `result` variable, which is a common tactic when matching compiler stack/register lifetimes.
Updated symbol names for spline `.sdata2` literals to match compiler-generated local literal labels, which the bot confirmed helped bring `.sdata2` to 100%.
decomp_lessons: For math-heavy baselib code, small storage-class changes (`static`, `inline`) can be decisive for matching even when source-level behavior is unchanged.
Header prototypes must follow linkage decisions: if a helper becomes `static`, remove its public header declaration to avoid exposing an internal function.
When matching bisection/integration routines, variable declaration order, const temporaries, and helper extraction can affect register allocation enough to matter.
Matching `.sdata2` may require using compiler-style `@NN` literal labels rather than descriptive address-based names, especially for floats/doubles emitted as local constants.
Only promote an object to `Matching` in `configure.py` after both code and related data literals are accounted for; here the bot reported `splArcLengthGetParameter` and `.sdata2` both reaching 100%.
naming_conventions: Existing public spline APIs use `splGet...` camel-case style, e.g. `splGetHelmite`, `splGetCardinalPoint`, `splGetBezierPoint`, `splGetSplinePoint`.
Internal helper names in this file may use an underscore after the `spl_` prefix, as seen with `spl_IterateSimpsonsMiddle`, `spl_GetCoeffs`, and the added `spl_GetArcLengthDx`.
Compiler-generated `.sdata2` literals are represented as `@NN` labels in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` when that better reflects the object layout than descriptive `HSD_Spline_804DE...` names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Toggled functions between plain, `inline`, and `static` definitions to influence emitted call/inlining behavior and symbol visibility.
Introduced a tiny helper around a single arithmetic expression to alter codegen in `splArcLengthGetParameter` without changing semantics.
Changed temporary variable usage from `const f32 midpoint` to assignment through `result`, affecting lifetime and reuse in the binary search loop.
Moved declarations for `dx` and `middle` before assignment rather than initializing them as consts, another source-shaping tactic for register allocation.
Aligned `.sdata2` symbol table entries with generated literal labels and preserved addresses, sizes, alignment, and float/double data annotations.
review_feedback: No human review comments or PR body rationale were present in the provided slice.
The only feedback evidence is the decomp.dev bot report: matched code increased by +912 bytes, linked code by +2268 bytes, matched data by +56 bytes, and two spline matches were reported.
follow_up_queries: Look for the successor or replacement PR for `mchen91:spline`, since this PR was closed with `merged_at` null.
Check whether later PRs kept `spl_GetArcLengthDx` or used a different final matching tactic for `splArcLengthGetParameter`.
Search later symbol updates around `.sdata2:0x804DE620` to confirm whether the `@NN` labels survived in master.
Verify whether `splGetCardinalPoint` and `splGetBezierPoint` remained header-declared while defined as `inline`, because this PR has no human review discussion explaining that choice.
